headline: Trump’s Iran Pivots Spotlight Washington’s Insider Trade Threat
summary: (Bloomberg) -- In the oil market, volatility recently spiked to levels not seen since the onset of the Covid-19 pandemic. Commodities and stocks globally have whipsawed in response to every twist and turn in the Iran war: A strike, a pause, a threat to wipe out a civilization, a ceasefire; then a deal, no deal, and the prospect of one again.Most Read from BloombergAnthropic’s Mythos Model Is Being Accessed by Unauthorized UsersInside Alex Cooper’s Unwell: Tears, Screaming and Employees Looking f

headline: CME : Treasury futures react as 10-Year yields push past 4.30%.
summary: 10-Year note yields pushed higher, peaking at 4.35% and closing north of 4.30%, marking an 8 bps increase for the week. The upward movement in rates was largely driven by ongoing geopolitical tensions...

headline: CME Q1 Deep Dive: Record Volumes Drive Growth Despite Revenue Shortfall
summary: Financial derivatives exchange CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 14.5% year on year to $1.88 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.36 per share was 0.6% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: CME Group Inc (CME) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Record-Breaking Volume and Revenue Surge
summary: CME Group Inc (CME) reports unprecedented growth with a 22% increase in average daily volume and a 14% rise in total revenue, despite facing regulatory and geopolitical challenges.
